about.title: About
about.subtitle: The Story Behind the Music
about.bio_paragraph_1: Alexandros Tsopozidis was born on January 1, 1986, in the village of Sameba (Guniakala), Georgia. Of Pontic Greek heritage, he grew up surrounded by the rich musical traditions of the Caucasus region, where Eastern melodies intertwined with Greek folk rhythms.
about.bio_paragraph_2: In 1998, at the age of 12, Alexandros moved with his family to Paphos, Cyprus, where he began a new chapter of his life. Initially drawn to athletics, he trained at a Cyprus football academy with dreams of a professional sports career.
about.bio_paragraph_3: At his parents' request, Alexandros left football behind and discovered his true calling — music. In 2011, he began his musical journey in earnest, and his talent quickly caught the attention of Russian producer Kemran Amirov, who helped launch his career.
about.bio_paragraph_4: His debut duet with Eldar Dalgatov, 'Mozhet Ty Vernyoshsya' (Maybe You'll Return), introduced him to Russian-speaking audiences. But it was his collaboration with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev on 'Brodyaga' (Wanderer) that catapulted him to fame, amassing over 69 million views on YouTube.
about.bio_paragraph_5: Today, Alexandros performs in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, blending pop with Caucasian and Eastern influences into a unique sound. Based in Paphos, Cyprus, he continues to release new music, with his latest single 'Kanitel' (2026) and 'Vechnaya Lyubov' (Eternal Love) coming soon. He is the recipient of the 9 Volna award for his contribution to ethnic music.
about.today_text: Based in Paphos, Cyprus. Latest release: "Kanitel" (2026). "Vechnaya Lyubov" (Eternal Love) coming soon.

press.title: Press Kit
press.subtitle: For media, event organizers, and booking agents
press.meta_description: Alexandros Tsopozidis press kit — bio, photos, booking info for media, wedding planners, and event organizers. Book for weddings, christenings & private events.
press.fact_genre: Greek-Eastern Pop, Caucasian Music, Pontic Greek
press.short_bio: Alexandros Tsopozidis is a Pontic Greek vocalist bridging the Caucasus, the Mediterranean, and the global stage. Singing in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, he fuses Eastern melodies with contemporary pop — a sound that earned him 69 million YouTube views on Brodyaga and the prestigious 9 Volna award for ethnic music.
press.medium_bio: Born in the village of Sameba, Georgia, and raised in Paphos, Cyprus, Alexandros Tsopozidis carries the soul of the Caucasus in every note. A Pontic Greek artist performing in three languages — Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek — he crafts a signature sound where Eastern tradition meets modern pop. His breakout collaboration "Brodyaga" (Wanderer) with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev surpassed 69 million YouTube views, while hits like "Male Male" (11M+ views) cemented his reputation across the Russian-speaking world. A 9 Volna award laureate, Alexandros commands festival stages from Moscow to Baku. Latest releases: "Kanitel" (2026), with "Vechnaya Lyubov" (Eternal Love) arriving soon.
press.full_bio:
Alexandros Tsopozidis is a genre-defying vocalist whose music bridges the ancient traditions of the Caucasus with the pulse of contemporary pop. Born on January 1, 1986, in the mountain village of Sameba (Guniakala), Georgia, he grew up steeped in the Pontic Greek musical heritage of his ancestors — a world where Eastern melodies and Greek folk rhythms exist as one.

At twelve, his family relocated to Paphos, Cyprus, where young Alexandros initially chased a career in professional football. That athletic discipline would later shape his relentless work ethic on stage. When he pivoted to music in 2011, Russian producer Kemran Amirov recognized his raw talent immediately, launching a career that would span continents and cultures.

His debut duet with Eldar Dalgatov, "Mozhet Ty Vernyoshsya" (Maybe You'll Return), introduced him to the Russian-speaking world. But it was "Brodyaga" (Wanderer), his collaboration with Elbrus Dzhanmirzoev, that became a cultural phenomenon — surpassing 69 million YouTube views and establishing Alexandros as one of the most distinctive voices in Eurasian pop. Subsequent hits including "Male Male" (11M+ views), "Armyane i Greki — Bratya Naveki" (Armenians and Greeks — Brothers Forever), and album "Za Toboy" (After You) reinforced his unique position at the crossroads of Greek, Caucasian, and Eastern European music.

A recipient of the 9 Volna award for contribution to ethnic music, Alexandros has headlined major festivals including Zhara (Baku), Karnavala (Gelendzhik), Kavkaz Music Fest, and "Zvyozdy Vostoka" (Stars of the East) with Vostok FM in Moscow.

Performing in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek, he continues to release music that honors tradition while pushing boundaries. His latest single "Kanitel" (2026) is out now, with "Vechnaya Lyubov" (Eternal Love) arriving soon. Based in Paphos, Cyprus.

why_book.title: Why Book Alexandros
why_book.subtitle: What makes him the right choice for your event
why_book.views_desc: Over 69 million views on Brodyaga alone, with a proven track record of audience engagement across his catalogue.
why_book.multilingual_desc: Performs in Russian, Greek, and Pontic Greek — connecting with diverse audiences across cultures.
why_book.event_types_desc: Perfect for weddings, christenings, baptisms, birthday parties, anniversaries, engagements, name days, corporate galas, and festivals of any scale.

why_book.custom_formats_desc: Flexible performance formats — from intimate acoustic sets to full-band concert productions.
